steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize how popular The Woman in White became. An effective answer must convey the scale or measurable impact of that popularity — not the technique behind it, the publication format, or the genre.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Woman in White became so popular that sales of the magazine it appeared in increased to a record-breaking 100,000 copies per issue': gives a concrete, quantified measure of how popular the novel was — directly achieves the goal. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Every installment ended with a moment of unresolved suspense, which led readers to eagerly anticipate the next week’s entry': explains the technique that drove popularity, not the popularity itself. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Collins’s novel was published in forty weekly installments between November 1859 and August 1860': describes the publication schedule, not how popular the novel was. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The Woman in White, a mystery novel, first appeared in a London literary magazine': identifies the genre and venue — no indication of popularity at all. ✗
Only A quantifies the novel’s popularity with a specific, record-breaking figure. That matches choice A — A is correct.
